text: this first step describes the opening of the fissure and the division of arterial branches so the fissure is open with a bipolar scissor in this case the fissure is thin
and its posterior part can be divided with a vessel sealing device.
This helps exposing the arterial branches to the posterior and upper segments.
They are controlled by clip and divided with a vessel sealing device.
Very arterial lymph nodes are dissected and removed.
Finally, the truncus anterior is approached from behind, dissected and stapled.
The upper lobe is pulled backward.
The vein is exposed and its lower root, which is the vein for the lingula, is respected.
A forceps is passed around the bronchial trunk but it is not yet clamped.
The whole left lung is reinflated
and then the forceps is closed.
Thus only the three upper segments remain inflated.
The demarcation line is clearly visible.
It is marked with coterie dots.
The bronchial trunk is now stapled.
A long forceps, which has been specially designed for this task, is applied on the demarcation line in order to compress the bronchioma and ease the application of the stapler.
The procedure is now completed and the specimen is retrieved.
